mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue acceleration, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of rising-INDPRO instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the restrictive monetary stance suppresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return rate falls below 50% when the trigger is active, or positive returns exceed the unconditional baseline by more than 5 percentage points over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate and deceleration in nonfarm payrolls signals labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC stays below 0.03 when both series are used together versus their univariate components, or the negative return rate is below 50% during joint signal periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during an economic expansion amplify construction and durable goods demand, lifting cyclical earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return rate is below 55% in mid-cycle expansion periods, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending that drags down corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return rate is below 55% following trigger events, or the average 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When accelerating inflation indicated by rising CPIAUCSL coincides with a rising federal funds rate, the combined squeeze on real margins and higher discount rates produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve Rank IC by at least 0.02 over the univariate FEDFUNDS signal over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor income and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the positive return rate is below 55% during payroll expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production growth rate below zero over a three-month window signals manufacturing recession and inventory liquidation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return rate is below 50% when the trigger is active, or the average 20-day return is positive during triggered periods over any 2-year rolling window.
